texto: Anything could be endured, she had discovered, if she could only package the time into discrete little packets. She imagined taking the minutes, each one like a pellet, and wrapping them up - one minute, five minutes, fifteen, thirty. Once she had managed to survive a full hour, she could put the packets of time into a box, tie it with string, and push it down a conveyor belt. Just one more minute, one more hour, one more day.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lynne Kutsukake: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Anything could be endured, she had discovered, if she could only package the time into discrete l…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, survival, time pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e time.
